meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Vocabulary Set 5
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Challenge set — antonyms, word families, feelings vs actions across all 16 words.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'beautiful'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'beautiful' (very pretty) is 'ugly' (not attractive). These are antonyms — words with opposite meanings.

questions.question: What is the opposite of 'protect'?
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'protect' (keep safe) is 'attack' or 'harm' (hurt). The tiger wanted to attack Chumpa; the farmer wanted to protect her.

questions.question: Match each word to its OPPOSITE.
questions.explanation: Antonyms: happy ↔ sad, truth ↔ lie, angry ↔ calm, beautiful ↔ ugly. These opposites help solidify your understanding of each word's meaning.

questions.question: 'Protect,' 'protection,' and 'protective' all come from the same root word.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Protect' (verb) → 'protection' (noun) → 'protective' (adjective). All three words come from the same Latin root meaning 'to cover' or 'to guard.'

questions.question: The opposite of 'surprised' is:
questions.explanation: The opposite of 'surprised' (shocked by something unexpected) is 'unsurprised' or 'expecting it' (you knew it was coming). If you expected the gift, you would not be surprised.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they BASE WORDS or CHANGED FORMS?
questions.explanation: 'Agree,' 'protect,' and 'beauty' are base words. 'Agreed' (past tense), 'protection' (noun form), and 'beautiful' (adjective from beauty) are changed forms.

questions.question: In the story, the tiger 'agreed' to the farmer's plan. This means:
options: The tiger understood the plan perfectly
The tiger said yes and accepted the conditions
The tiger was surprised by the farmer
questions.explanation: 'Agreed' means said yes and accepted. The tiger agreed to the farmer's conditions — he said yes to the deal without fully understanding it. This is how the farmer outsmarted him.

questions.question: The tiger 'promised' not to attack, but then he attacked anyway — this means he broke his promise.
questions.explanation: Yes! When you break a promise, you do the opposite of what you said you would do. The tiger promised not to attack, then attacked anyway — he broke his word.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a sentence about the story's lesson:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Cleverness can protect against brute strength.' The farmer was physically weaker than the tiger but used intelligence to protect Chumpa — cleverness won.

questions.question: Sort these words into POSITIVE (good/helpful) and NEGATIVE (bad/harmful).
questions.explanation: 'Protect' (keeps safe), 'beautiful' (pleasing), 'truth' (honest), and 'graze' (peaceful activity) are positive. 'Attack' (hurts) and 'angry' (negative emotion) are negative words.

questions.question: Which sentence uses BOTH 'anger' and 'protect' correctly in a meaningful way?
options: A mother's anger can protect her children from danger.
The anger grassy was beautiful and surprised.
He promised to anger and protect the tiger.
questions.explanation: A mother's 'anger' (strong emotion) when her child is threatened can motivate her to 'protect' them (keep them safe). Both words are used correctly and meaningfully.

questions.question: 'Angry,' 'surprised,' 'happy,' 'sad,' and 'beautiful' are all adjectives because they describe how a person or thing is.
questions.explanation: Yes! All five words are adjectives. 'Angry,' 'surprised,' 'happy,' and 'sad' describe feelings. 'Beautiful' describes appearance. They all describe what something is like.

questions.question: The farmer's plan worked because the tiger was _______ — he was honest but not clever enough to understand the trick.
questions.hint: The tiger believed the farmer's words without suspicion
questions.explanation: The tiger was 'trusting' or 'honest' — he took the farmer at his word without suspecting a trick. This is why cleverness defeated strength in the story.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence using 'shadowy' and 'forest':
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The tiger watched from the shadowy forest.' The tiger hid among the dark trees and watched the cow — 'shadowy forest' sets a tense, threatening scene.

questions.question: The tiger was strong but the farmer was clever. Which vocabulary word BEST describes what the farmer did to protect Chumpa?
options: He used 'truth' cleverly — tricking the tiger with honest-sounding words
He 'grazed' with the tiger to make peace
He 'promised' to be the tiger's friend
questions.explanation: The farmer used 'truth' — or technically clever wording that was not quite the truth — to confuse the tiger. He protected Chumpa through wit, not force.
